DECLARATION OF CHRISTINA Y. CHEN IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IN FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SUN, TIM CHOI , HUSBAND SUN, MARY MOU CHENG , WIFE
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-23-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ELAINE TAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION - ANTI-SLAPP IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. AT MOST, THE COMPLAINT ARISING OUT OF REPUDIATION OF TRUST, IS EVIDENCED BY THE CLAIM REJECTION FILED IN A PROBATE PROCEEDING. "THE APPROPRIATE COURSE OF ACTION WHEN A CREDITOR'S CLAIM IS REJECTED IS FOR THE CREDITOR TO COMMENCE A SEPARATE ACTION ON THE REJECTED CLAIM CHALLENGING REJECTION OF THE CREDITOR'S CLAIM. (MILLER, SUPRA, 15 CAL.APP.2D AT PP. 614-615, 59 P.2D 1035; 9250, 9252.) THIS IS THE EXCLUSIVE METHOD OF ENFORCING A CLAIM WHICH HAS BEEN REJECTED BY THE COURT." MCDONALD V. STRUCTURED ASSET SALES, LLC (2007) 154 CAL.APP.4TH 1068. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM AND LEAVE THE PORTION ON SANCTIONS BLANK. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: KIM THORPE, CSR	318 (415-421-3021) REPORTED (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

Notice Of Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint Third Cause Of Action - Anti-Slapp
Off Calendar Sep-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christina Chen, Esq (415-441-8778) for Plaintiff and opposing party Tim Choi Sun.
Gerard Launay, Esq (510-486-1000) for Defendant and moving party Elain Tan.
Court Reporter: Kim Thorpe of Atkinson Baker, CSR 318, (415-421-3021), Reported.

Argued. Hearing is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ELAINE TAN's Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint Third Cause Of Action - Anti-Slapp is DENIED. Plaintiff's third cause of action does not arise out of protected activity. At most, the complaint arising out of repudiation of trust, is evidenced by the claim rejection filed in a probate proceeding. "The appropriate course of action when a creditor's claim is rejected is for the creditor to commence a separate action on the rejected claim challenging rejection of the creditor's claim. (Miller, supra, 15 Cal.App.2d at pp. 614-615, 59 P.2d 1035; 9250, 9252.) This is the exclusive method of enforcing a claim which has been rejected by the court." McDonald v. Structured Asset Sales, LLC (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 1068. Prevailing party to submit order approved as to form and leave the portion on sanctions blank. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Kim Thorpe, CSR 318 (415-421-3021) Reported (D501)
